sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच
योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन।
एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात्स्थितिं स्थिराम्॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
yo ’yaṁ yogas tvayā proktaḥ sāmyena madhusūdana |
etasyāhaṁ na paśyāmi cañcalatvāt sthitiṁ sthirām ||
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: This yoga proclaimed by You, O Madhusūdana, I do not see a stable establishment for it, because of the mind’s restlessness.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna voices the universal fear that the mind is too turbulent to be stilled, collapsing the ideal of meditative equipoise into felt impossibility.
psychological_state: Cognitive overload and learned-helplessness toward internal regulation; a defensive surrender before trial.
trigger_scenario: A client abandons a meditation app after three days, saying "My brain just won’t shut up—this isn’t for people like me."
anchor_text: Notice the mind’s waves without quitting the effort to steady it.
